title: Seattle's Top 10 Must-See Attractions for First Time Visitors | Explore Seattle Southside
content: # Seattle's Top 10 Must-See Attractions for First Time Visitors. Or sightseeing in the beautiful Seattle area for the first time? Whether you're a first-time visitor, a frequent traveler, or a local rediscovering the beauty of the Pacific Northwest, Seattle offers an array of iconic attractions you won’t want to miss. Since 1907, the iconic Pike Place Market has been a cornerstone of Seattle’s history and a top tourist attraction for visitors worldwide. For convenient transportation, take the Link Light Rail from Seattle-Tacoma International Airport (SEA) directly to downtown Seattle (Westlake Center), it's just a short walk from the market. Located in the heart of Seattle Center, this area is a must-visit for tourists with plenty of attractions nearby. The Museum of Flight, one of the largest air and space museums in the world, offers an immersive experience in aviation history and technology. Whether you're looking to explore skydiving for the first time or sharpen your skills, iFly promises an incredible experience in Seattle Southside.

title: Seattle, Washington: Best Things to See, Do and Experience - Food, Wine & Travel
content: Seattle View from The Space Needle. # Seattle, Washington: Best Things to See, Do and Experience. Seattle, the largest city in Washington and the entire Pacific Northwest, offers a plethora of attractions and experiences, some worthy of revisiting again and again. Following my second trip to Seattle last fall, I compiled my list of the best things to see, do, and experience there to help others with their trip planning. I had looked forward to spending time at Pike Place Market again and to finally being able to go to the top of the Space Needle, which was undergoing renovation on my 2018 visit. I was not only able to do those things but had new experiences and added others to the list of things I look forward to discovering on a future visit. **Tip: Take the Monorail to the Seattle Center –**Three of Seattle’s top attractions — the Seattle Space Needle, Chihuly Garden and Glass, and Museum of Pop Culture (MoPOP) are located at the Seattle Center.

title: 25 Top Things to Do in Seattle (Must See Attractions)
content: The open-air MarketFront plaza offers fantastic views of Elliott Bay, and [Overlook Walk](https://waterfrontseattle.org/waterfront-projects/overlook-walk)—which opened in 2024—is a beautifully landscaped elevated park that connects Pike Place to the Seattle Aquarium and the city’s newly redesigned waterfront. It’s home to nearly 20 large-scale installations and offers sweeping views of Elliott Bay. Don’t miss exploring SAM’s impressive Asian art collection at the [Seattle Asian Art Museum](https://www.seattleartmuseum.org/visit/seattle-asian-art-museum), located amid the greenery of Capitol Hill’s beautiful Volunteer Park. In the always bustling [Lake Union](https://visitseattle.org/neighborhoods/south-lake-union/) neighborhood, just northeast of downtown, you can view seaplanes and boats of all kinds, including the floating home from *Sleepless in Seattle*. Much of the action is centered on 12-acre [Lake Union Park](https://www.seattle.gov/parks/allparks/lake-union-park), which is home to compelling interactive exhibits at the 50,000-square-foot [Museum of History & Industry (MOHAI)](https://mohai.org/). Several meticulously preserved early 1900s performance spaces in downtown Seattle present first-rate plays, musicals, and concerts, including the city’s oldest venue, the [Moore Theatre](https://www.stgpresents.org/stg-venues/moore-theatre/), which opened in 1907.

If you're a first-time visitor to Seattle, no trip would be complete without stopping by some of the city's most iconic attractions. Browse the bustling stalls of fresh produce and flowers that make up Pike Place Market and take in. Though Pike Place Market is one of the most tourist-heavy attractions in Seattle (plan to run into crowds, especially on the weekends) that's no reason to scratch it off your to-do list. The streets surrounding Pike Place Market are peppered with restaurants and coffee shops, and there's an information booth just west of the marketplace at First Avenue. **U.S. News Insider Tip:** Utilize the museum’s interactive mobile guide, which provides a map, schedules of live talks and demos as well as an audio guide for insight into the pieces that make up the exhibition.

title: 15 Best Things to Do in Seattle (2026 Guide)
content: # 15 Best Things to Do in Seattle (2026 Guide). ***This is our insider scoop on the 15 best things to do in Seattle!***. We’ve visited the ‘Emerald City’ a couple of times now, as we live in nearby B.C. And we’re here to tell you that **Seattle** isn’t just about rain and coffee (although both those things are, funnily enough, why we like this city so much). As I said, we’ve visited this cool city several times, so we’ve tried out pretty much everything there is to do in Seattle for tourists, and we’ve included all those below, as well as some **hidden gem spots**, which are actually our favorite parts of the city. In the guide below, you’ll still read about insanely popular attractions like the Seattle Space Needle and Pike Place Market. First up on our list of the best things to do in Seattle is the iconic and hard-to-miss Space Needle (which is a whopping 605 feet tall!).
